title: Evals Are Broken, Use Them Anyway — Ara Khan, Cline
summary: Ara Khan argues that AI benchmarks are flawed but useful, and proposes a three-zone framework for analyzing benchmark failures: obvious bugs, model-specific nuance improvements, and overfitting artifacts. He demonstrates that improvements come from prompt engineering and infrastructure tuning rather than model selection alone.
